records.ety: [OE. wildernesse, wilderne,probably from AS. wildor a wild beast; cf. D. wildernis wilderness. See Wilder, v. t.]

defs: A tract of land, or a region, uncultivated and uninhabited by human beings, whether a forest or a wide, barren plain; a wild; a waste; a desert; a pathless waste of any kind.
quotes: The wat'ry wilderness yields no supply.

defs: Quality or state of being wild; wildness.
quotes: These paths and bowers doubt not but our joint hands. Will keep from wilderness with ease.
